page: Microsoft Azure D2as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. It features 8.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This server does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile. The single-core CPU capabilities are strong, with the stress-ng single-core test and Geekbench single-core score both placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance is limited due to the 2-vCPU hardware constraint, with stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck; the small-block read-only memory bandwidth is in the bottom 10%, and the large-block read-only bandwidth is in the bottom 25%. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show average performance.

The primary tradeoff of the D2as_v6 is its strong single-threaded execution contrasted against weak multi-threaded and memory-bound performance. From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a low-cost entry point for workloads that do not require high memory bandwidth or parallel processing. It is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, text processing, and small-scale development or testing environments that can leverage its strong single-core speed without saturating its limited memory and multi-core resources.
description: Microsoft Azure D2as_v6 is an entry-level general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, with no local storage or GPU. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% for single-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are weak, with small-block read-only memory bandwidth falling into the bottom 10%. This resource profile makes the instance a cost-effective option for single-threaded tasks, basic text processing, and lightweight web serving, while representing a clear tradeoff for memory-intensive or highly parallelized workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2as_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, 2 vCPUs, and 8 GB RAM. It offers strong single-core performance but exhibits limited multi-core and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2as_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 2 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, offering strong single-core performance but limited memory bandwidth.
tagline: An entry-level general-purpose virtual machine featuring strong single-core AMD EPYC performance paired with balanced memory allocation for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%
No local storage or GPU, but supports Premium Storage
Weak multi-core performance and low memory bandwidth
Suitable for lightweight web serving, text processing, and basic testing
